findings.title: 401 session-expiry re-persists the logged-out user's trips to localStorage (privacy wipe defeated by the same handler's emit)
findings.rootCause: clearAuthToken() (api/core.ts:82) does localStorage.removeItem('theGreatEscapeState') and resets a SUBSET of STATE (notifications, draftExpense, geminiApiKey, settlements, achievements, lastImportBatch, preferences, rateMode, insightCurrency) but NOT the heavy domain arrays (STATE.trips/expenses/budgets/tripDays/archivedTrips/categories). The 401 branch in apiFetch (api/core.ts:269-284) calls clearAuthToken() then STATE.user=null then emit(EVENTS.STATE_CHANGED). The default subscriber `subscribe(EVENTS.STATE_CHANGED, saveState)` (state.ts:438) schedules a debounced _doSave() (state.ts:353-367) which JSON.stringify(STATE)s the WHOLE object — including the trips still in memory — back into the key clearAuthToken just removed. The full logout path (pages/profile.ts:70-89) clears these arrays itself, which is why it's safe; the 401 + restoreSession()-failure callers of clearAuthToken (api/core.ts:275, bootstrap/auth.ts:193) rely on clearAuthToken to do the wipe, and it doesn't finish the job.

findings.title: Trip-media hydration Maps (_mediaLoadedTrips/_mediaVersion/_pendingMedia) survive logout → shared-device media loss + hidden media on shared trips
findings.rootCause: _mediaLoadedTrips (Set), _mediaVersion (Map) and _pendingMedia (Map) are module-level singletons in api/media.ts and are NEVER cleared on logout — clearAuthToken() (api/core.ts:82) and pages/profile.ts logout reset STATE + the SW cache but never call .clear() on these. They key purely by tripId, with no user binding, so a tripId User A loaded stays 'loaded' for User B. Two consequences chain: (a) pullFromServer's cold-hydrate guard (api.ts:317) treats T as already hydrated for B and never GETs the real media, leaving B's STATE on the []-placeholder; (b) persistTripMedia's safety gate (media.ts:274), which exists specifically to stop []-placeholders from clobbering server media, is bypassed because the stale set says T is hydrated — so the placeholder write proceeds.

findings.title: Warm-path 409 union-merge resurrects a peer device's deleted media item
findings.rootCause: _postTripMedia's 409 recovery (media.ts:139-144) re-merges via _mergeMediaField (media.ts:62-73), a pure union-by-key that keeps any key present on EITHER side. The cold/parked path's loss-free claim relies on 'the only possible offline edit is an ADD' (media.ts:46), but the warm 409 path runs on a fully-hydrated trip where a DELETE is a normal edit. Union-by-key cannot represent a deletion, so a concurrent delete always loses to any concurrent edit. There is no tombstone/per-item version to distinguish 'never had it' from 'deleted it'.

findings.title: Per-trip POI preference keys (enabledPois / pillEpicenters / poiAnchoring) are never pruned when a trip is deleted
findings.rootCause: The permanent-delete handler (trip-controls.ts:174-204) prunes expenses, tripDays, settlements, budgets, and STATE.fxOverridesByTrip[trip.id] (line 192) but NOT STATE.preferences.enabledPois[trip.id] / pillEpicenters[trip.id]. The archived-trip permanent-delete path (collections/handlers.ts:94) prunes none of the per-trip preference maps at all. There is no boot-time reconciliation that drops preference keys whose tripId no longer exists in STATE.trips/archivedTrips (loadState only backfills/heals these maps, never garbage-collects stale keys — state.ts:189-207).

findings.title: cpiCache / rateCache are persisted but not validated or non-finite-healed on load (NaN can reach the inflation factor), unlike the sibling money maps
findings.rootCause: validateLoadedState (schemas.ts:198-225) only shape-checks top-level arrays + activeTripId + user; it does not inspect rateCache/cpiCache. loadState() adds explicit non-finite healing loops for STATE.fxOverridesByTrip (state.ts:146-165) and STATE.manualRates (state.ts:170-188) — with a comment that a corrupt override 'could carry NaN/Infinity into the Insights calc and poison every total' — but cpiCache/rateCache, which feed the identical calc, get no equivalent heal. The runtime guards in presentValue.ts cover the override (ovValid, IA-1) and manual (Number.isFinite(manualPct)) branches but not the auto CPI branch, where makeInflationFactor only guards latestVal !== 0, not interior values.
